title: Discounted Internet Service - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Discounted Internet Service 7 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CHARTER SPECTRUM SPECTRUM INTERNET ASSIST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers discounted high speed internet access to households where Spectrum is available. Service includes modem, high speed internet up 50 Mbps, no data caps, and no contract. 400 Atlantic Street Floor 10 Stamford, CT 06901 Eligibility: -Must not be a current subscriber to Spectrum internet services -One or more members of household must be a recipient of one of the following assistance programs: National School Lunch Program (NSLP) Community Eligibility Provision (CEP) of the NSLP Supplemental Security Income (for applicants age 65+ only) Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (855) 366-7132 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF HILLSBORO HILIGHT BRIDGE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides discounted service for the City of Hillsboro HiLight Internet. Qualifying customers receive 1 gigabit internet service for $10 per month for 12 consecutive months with option to renew. Installation included for new customers. 150 E Main Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Residents qualify if they meet one of the following criteria: Documented total household income is below 185% of the federal poverty level OR At least one member of the household is enrolled in and receiving benefits from any of the following government assistance programs: SNAP, TANF, OHP, HUD Housing Voucher, LIEAP, or WIC Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 693-4400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INFORMATIONAL RECORD SCHOOL SUPPLIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Many schools receive assistance through school supply drives and corporate donations. Assistance may include backpacks, laptops, or Chromebooks for at-home use during the school year, home internet access/ hot spot assistance, and school supplies such as pencils, crayons, and notebooks. Assistance varies by the school district and funding availability and is not guaranteed. Parents or caregivers can contact the child's school or school district to find out if they offer school supplies for students in need. Families experiencing homelessness can also contact the McKinney-Vento homeless liaison at their school. More Details OREGON PUBLIC UTILITY COMMISSION OREGON LIFELINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a monthly discount on phone or high-speed internet service. Eligible households may: -Receive a discount on their phone bill of up to $20.25 per month; -Receive a discount on their high-speed internet of up to $24.25 per month; or -Receive free wireless voice minutes and data service Discount amounts are subject to change. Federal and state regulations limit the Lifeline benefit to one person per household for phone or high-speed internet service. 201 High Street SE Suite 100 Salem, OR 973013398 Eligibility: Individuals age 18 and older who receive, or have a member in their household who receives one of the following benefits, and whose total household income is at or below 135 percent of Federal Poverty Level (FPL): -Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) -Medicaid -Supplemental Security Income (SSI) -Federal Public Housing Assistance -Veterans or Survivors Pension Benefit -Bureau of Indian Affairs General Assistance -Tribal Temporary Assistance for Needy Families -Food Distribution Program on Indian Reservations Tribal Head Start (only households that meet the same qualifying standard) Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm. Closed on state recognized holidays. Email (503) 373-7171 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON PUBLIC UTILITY COMMISSION TRIBAL PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a reduction in the monthly cost or installation of phone or broadband service. The Tribal Lifeline program offers a $25 discount per month. The Tribal Link Up program provides a one-time discount of up to $100 on the initial installation or activation of wireline or wireless phone service. Federal and state regulations limit the Lifeline benefit to one person per household for phone or high-speed internett service. 201 High Street SE Suite 100 Salem, OR 973013398 Eligibility: Individuals and households at or below 135% of the Federal Poverty level or who are receiving one of the following benefits: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), Supplemental Security Income (SSI), Medicaid, Federal Public Housing Assistance, or Veterans/Survivors Pension benefits who live on the federally-recognized tribal lands which are as follows: -Burns Paiute Tribe -Confederated Tribes of Coos, Lower Umpqua, and Siuslaw Indians -Confederated Tribes of Grande Ronde -Confederated Tribes of Siletz Indians -Confederated Tribes of Warm Springs -Coquille Indian Tribe -Cow Creek Band of Umpqua Tribe of Indians -Klamath Tribes Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm. Closed on state recognized holidays. Email (503) 373-7171 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PCS FOR PEOPLE BRIDGING THE GAP DISCOUNTED TECHNOLOGY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers 5G and 4G LTE internet service and a discount modem. Modem will be shipped and should arrive in 3-5 business days. 1481 Marshall Avenue Saint Paul, MN 55104 Eligibility: Must be below 200% Federal Poverty Level, be below 60% of Area Median Income, or be currently enrolled in an income-based government assistance program Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (651) 354-2552 Get Directions Visit Website More Details T-MOBILE PROJECT 10MILLION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides internet access. Enrollees receive a hotspot data access point and 200GB of data per year for a 5 year period. Recertification is not required. 12920 SE 38th Street Bellevue, WA 98006 Eligibility: Families with children enrolled in one of the following government programs: -National School Lunch Program (NSLP) -Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) -Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT or P-EBT) -Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) -Community Eligibility Provision (CEP) -Food Distribution Program on Indian Reservations (FDPIR) -Medicaid -Head Start, along with foster youth, migrant, homeless, or runaway youth Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Get Directions Visit Website More Details XFINITY INTERNET ESSENTIALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides low-cost internet connection, computers, and an online learning center offering basic skills, online safety and security tips, resources, and internet classes. 1701 John F Kennedy Boulevard Philadelphia, PA 19103 Eligibility: -Receive one of the following public assistance programs: Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, WIC, LIHEAP, SSI, public housing assistance (HUD or HAP), Federal Pell Grant, Headstart, National School Lunch Program, or tribal assistance -Not a current Xfinity Internet customer or within the last 90 days -No outstanding Comcast debt less than a year old Hours: Not applicable Email (855) 846-8376 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF HILLSBORO HILIGHT BRIDGE PROGRAM OREGON PUBLIC UTILITY COMMISSION OREGON LIFELINE
